subject: Accounting
title: Accounts Receivable Management
description: A quick win: understand Accounts Receivable Management.

hook.text: You sold $50,000 worth of goods on credit. Now the hard part: collecting the money.

buildup.text: Accounts receivable tracks money customers owe you—each invoice, due date, and aging period.

discovery.text: An aging report groups receivables by how overdue they are—30, 60, 90, 120+ days.

twist.text: The longer a receivable ages, the less likely it is to be collected. Time is the enemy.

climax.text: Proactive follow-up on overdue accounts keeps cash flowing and bad debts under control.

punchline.text: A sale isn't complete until the cash arrives.

quiz.question: What does an aging report show?
options: How long receivables have been outstanding
